metadata.note: Statements derived from senaite-1.3 VEX where the actual senaite-2.3 grype scan confirms the CVE; senaite-2.3-specific statements added on top. python-2.7-rooted findings cross-reference common/images/python-2.7/python-2.7.vex.json via the 'Inherited from python-2.7' prose pattern.

statements.impact_statement: High-severity Pillow 6.2.2 image-parsing finding. Pillow is the server-side image processor for sample uploads (microscopy, gels) by authenticated lab technicians. senaite.core 2.3.0 pins Pillow at 6.2.2 — the last Python-2.7-compatible release; newer security fixes are not back-portable without dropping Py2 support. The upload pipeline accepts files from authenticated users, so the attack surface is real. PRINCIPAL-REVIEW-BLOCKING until the upload pipeline's MIME whitelist, size cap, and processing-isolation strategy are documented for the senaite-2.3 deployment.

PRINCIPAL-REVIEW-BLOCKING: Medium-severity jquery 1.12.4 finding in browser-side JavaScript shipped by Plone 5.2.9 / senaite.lims 2.3.0 static resources. The vulnerable code only executes in the operator's browser session against trusted SENAITE-rendered markup; SENAITE deployments are operator-only (no anonymous or untrusted users) per VEX-POLICY.md. Pending principal review of CSP and operator threat model.

statements.status_notes: werkzeug 1.0.1 is a Flask-stack WSGI utility; SENAITE 2.3.0 runs on Zope 4.x's classic ZServer (bin/instance, WSGI off), not Flask or any WSGI bridge. werkzeug is in the SBOM as a transitive dep of Plone-test or zope.interface support code, but its HTTP request/response objects are never instantiated at runtime — Plone 5.2.9 uses Zope's own ZPublisher exclusively. The senaite-2.3 image follows the same constraint: there is no waitress or WSGI optionality in the buildout.

The vulnerable code only executes in the operator's browser session against trusted SENAITE-rendered markup; SENAITE deployments are operator-only (no anonymous or untrusted users) per VEX-POLICY.md. Pending principal review of CSP and operator threat model.

statements.status_notes: certifi 2021.10.8 ships as the requests/urllib3 trust store. The advisory covers a CA root included in the bundle. SENAITE LIMS deployments only make outbound TLS connections to operator-configured endpoints (admin sets the URL when configuring senaite.jsonapi/REST integrations), and the runtime never receives connections from arbitrary remote TLS servers — there is no path through which an attacker controls the server presenting a compromised-CA-issued certificate to senaite-lims-2.3. Heritage Python 2.7 cannot upgrade certifi past 2021.10.8 (requests 2.27.1's last 2.7-compatible pin).

setuptools 44.1.1 is inherited from the python-2.7 base image (last 2.7-compatible release) and used at senaite-lims-2.3 build time only (zc.buildout pre-installs transitive deps). The runtime entrypoint is /opt/senaite/bin/instance (Plone Zope2 server); the image filesystem is read-only and there is no scheduler or shell that exec's setuptools. The vulnerable code path never fires on the deployed container. See common/images/python-2.7/python-2.7.vex.json for the same finding at the lower layer.

High-severity RestrictedPython 5.2 finding. RestrictedPython is Zope's sandboxing primitive — it compiles operator-authored Through-The-Web Python (TTW scripts) into a constrained AST. The senaite-2.3 image pins RestrictedPython 5.2 (the senaite.core 2.3.0 / Plone 5.2.9 baseline). Exploit primitives require write access to portal_skins/custom or Script (Python) objects, which is restricted to Manager-role accounts; SENAITE deployments lock this down per VEX-POLICY.md. PRINCIPAL-REVIEW-BLOCKING for confirmation that no additional TTW endpoints are exposed in the deployment.

statements.status_notes: certifi 2021.10.8 ships as the requests/urllib3 trust store. The advisory covers a CA root included in the bundle. SENAITE LIMS deployments only make outbound TLS connections to operator-configured endpoints (admin sets the URL when configuring senaite.jsonapi/REST integrations), and the runtime never receives connections from arbitrary remote TLS servers — there is no path through which an attacker controls the server presenting a compromised-CA-issued certificate to senaite-lims-2.3. Heritage Python 2.7 cannot upgrade certifi past 2021.10.8 (requests 2.27.1's last 2.7-compatible pin).

PRINCIPAL-REVIEW-BLOCKING: High-severity libexpat1 2.7.1-2 finding. libexpat is consumed by Python's pyexpat for XML parsing. SENAITE/Plone use lxml (libxml2) for primary XML parsing; pyexpat sees occasional internal use through stdlib (e.g. plistlib, XML-RPC). Pending principal review of whether any reachable XML-parsing path is fed adversary-controlled input — defaulting to under_investigation for the senaite-2.3 image since the audit has not yet been completed.

High-severity Plone 5.2.9 finding. Plone 5.2.x has its own hotfix series (Products.PloneHotfix4_3_18 and earlier 4.3.x hotfixes) — distinct from the Plone 5.2.x hotfix series referenced in the senaite-2.3 image's VEX. The senaite-lims-2.3 buildout currently pins NO Plone 5.2 hotfix package. PRINCIPAL-REVIEW-BLOCKING until the latest applicable Plone 5.2 hotfix is pinned in buildout/versions.cfg and this CVE's coverage by that hotfix is confirmed.

See common/images/python-2.7/python-2.7.vex.json for the lower-layer statement.

statements.status_notes: Inherited from python-2.7.vex.json. ASLR-bypass disclosure; upstream states ASLR weakening is not itself a vulnerability. Exploitation requires a separate primitive (setuid binary or exposed listener) that this image does not provide. The senaite-lims-2.3 image runs the Plone bin/instance HTTP server on top of the same python-2.7 base, with no additional code path that reaches the vulnerable glibc primitive. See common/images/python-2.7/python-2.7.vex.json for the lower-layer statement.

statements.impact_statement: PRINCIPAL-REVIEW-BLOCKING: Medium-severity liblzma5 5.8.1-1 finding. lzma is consumed by Python's lzma module. SENAITE/Plone never invoke lzma at runtime — no archive-decoding endpoint exists.

PRINCIPAL-REVIEW-BLOCKING: High-severity lxml 4.8.0 finding. lxml is the libxml2/libxslt binding used by Plone's transform pipeline (XML import/export, RSS, PDF rendering). senaite.core 2.3.0 pins lxml at 4.8.0 — the last release that builds cleanly against Python 2.7. The vulnerable code paths require attacker-controlled XML; SENAITE's XML inputs are operator-supplied via authenticated import flows, not anonymous endpoints. Pending principal review of which lxml entry points are reachable without operator credentials.
Negligible-severity libxslt1.1 1.1.35-1.2+deb13u2 finding. libxslt is consumed by lxml's XSLT processor. Same review context as the lxml findings.

statements.status_notes: certifi 2021.10.8 ships as the requests/urllib3 trust store. The advisory covers a CA root included in the bundle. SENAITE LIMS deployments only make outbound TLS connections to operator-configured endpoints (admin sets the URL when configuring senaite.jsonapi/REST integrations), and the runtime never receives connections from arbitrary remote TLS servers — there is no path through which an attacker controls the server presenting a compromised-CA-issued certificate to senaite-lims-2.3. Heritage Python 2.7 cannot upgrade certifi past 2021.10.8 (requests 2.27.1's last 2.7-compatible pin).

Server-side SVG rendering against authenticated-only LIMS report data is the relevant deployment path; pending principal review of whether report templates can embed externally-fetched URI references and the CairoSVG version's url_fetcher configuration. Same review context as the senaite-2.3 image's cairosvg statements — both versions are the last 2.7-compatible release.

statements.status_notes: Inherited from python-2.7.vex.json. glob() recursion limit; documented POSIX behaviour and Debian flags this as unimportant. SENAITE never invokes glob() against attacker-supplied patterns — Plone catalog queries use Zope's own ZCatalog query language, not C-library globbing. The senaite-lims-2.3 image runs the Plone bin/instance HTTP server on top of the same python-2.7 base, with no additional code path that reaches the vulnerable glibc primitive. See common/images/python-2.7/python-2.7.vex.json for the lower-layer statement.

werkzeug 1.0.1 is a Flask-stack WSGI utility; SENAITE 2.3.0 runs on Zope 4.x's classic ZServer (bin/instance, WSGI off), not Flask or any WSGI bridge. werkzeug is in the SBOM as a transitive dep of Plone-test or zope.interface support code, but its HTTP request/response objects are never instantiated at runtime — Plone 5.2.9 uses Zope's own ZPublisher exclusively. The senaite-2.3 image follows the same constraint: there is no waitress or WSGI optionality in the buildout.

Inherited from python-2.7.vex.json. Requires the local user to run `ldd` on a malicious ELF; glibc upstream classifies this as non-security. The senaite-lims-2.3 runtime is distroless (no shell, no `ldd`); an interactive caller cannot execute the vulnerable program. The senaite-lims-2.3 image runs the Plone bin/instance HTTP server on top of the same python-2.7 base, with no additional code path that reaches the vulnerable glibc primitive. See common/images/python-2.7/python-2.7.vex.json for the lower-layer statement.

statements.status_notes: pip 20.3.4 is inherited from the python-2.7 base image (last 2.7-compatible release) and used at senaite-lims-2.3 build time only (zc.buildout pre-installs transitive deps). Negligible-severity libxml2 2.12.7+dfsg+really2.9.14-2.1+deb13u2 finding. libxml2 is consumed by lxml. Inherits the same review context as the lxml findings — pending principal review of whether SENAITE invokes XML parsing against untrusted input.
Negligible-severity libblkid1 2.41-5 finding. libblkid is part of util-linux's block-device probing. The senaite-lims-2.3 runtime never probes block devices — no exec path invokes blkid.

Medium-severity Pillow 6.2.2 image-parsing finding. Pillow is the server-side image processor for sample uploads (microscopy, gels) by authenticated lab technicians. senaite.core 2.3.0 pins Pillow at 6.2.2 — the last Python-2.7-compatible release; newer security fixes are not back-portable without dropping Py2 support. The upload pipeline accepts files from authenticated users, so the attack surface is real. PRINCIPAL-REVIEW-BLOCKING until the upload pipeline's MIME whitelist, size cap, and processing-isolation strategy are documented for the senaite-2.3 deployment.
